signs: Talking about wanting to die or kill oneself
Expressing feelings of hopelessness or having no reason to live
Talking about feeling trapped or in unbearable pain
Talking about being a burden to others
Sleeping too little or too much
Searching online for methods of self-harm
Sudden calmness after a period of depression
Loss of interest in things previously enjoyed
Feelings of shame, humiliation, or despair
Feeling like a burden to others

deEscalationTechniques.description: Give your full attention, reflect back what you hear, and validate their feelings without judgment.
steps: Use reflective statements: 'It sounds like you're feeling...'
Validate their pain: 'That sounds incredibly difficult.'
Avoid minimizing: Don't say 'It's not that bad' or 'Just think positive.'

deEscalationTechniques.description: Help the person reconnect with the present moment through sensory awareness.
steps: Guide them through the 5-4-3-2-1 technique (5 things you see, 4 you hear, 3 you touch, 2 you smell, 1 you taste).
Encourage slow, deep breathing (breathe in for 4, hold for 4, out for 6).
Ask them to hold a comforting object or press feet firmly to the ground.

deEscalationTechniques.description: Collaborate on a concrete plan to keep the person safe.
steps: List internal coping strategies they can use.
Identify people and social settings that provide distraction.
List people they can contact for help.
List professional resources and crisis lines.
Make the environment safe by reducing access to lethal means.

safetyPlanningSteps.title: Recognize Warning Signs
safetyPlanningSteps.description: Identify thoughts, images, moods, situations, and behaviors that precede a crisis.
prompts: What thoughts tell you a crisis may be developing?
What mood changes do you notice before things get worse?
What situations tend to trigger these feelings?

safetyPlanningSteps.title: Internal Coping Strategies
safetyPlanningSteps.description: List things you can do on your own to take your mind off problems and help you resist acting on thoughts of self-harm.
prompts: What activities help you feel better or distract you?
What relaxation techniques work for you?
What gives you a sense of purpose or accomplishment?

safetyPlanningSteps.title: Social Contacts for Distraction
safetyPlanningSteps.description: Identify people and social settings that can provide positive distraction.
prompts: Who can you be around to feel better (even without discussing the crisis)?
What social settings help you feel connected?

safetyPlanningSteps.title: People to Contact for Help
safetyPlanningSteps.description: List specific people you can reach out to when you need help.
prompts: Who are the people you trust most?
Who can you call at any time of day or night?
Have you talked to any of these people about how they can help?

safetyPlanningSteps.title: Professional Resources
safetyPlanningSteps.description: List professional contacts and crisis services.
prompts: Who is your therapist or counselor?
What is the nearest emergency room?
Do you have the crisis hotline numbers saved in your phone?

safetyPlanningSteps.title: Making the Environment Safe
safetyPlanningSteps.description: Reduce access to lethal means and create a safer environment.
prompts: Are there things in your environment that could be dangerous during a crisis?
Can you ask someone to hold onto these items for you?
Is there a safe space you can go to?

emergencyResources.name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
emergencyResources.description: Free and confidential emotional support for people in suicidal crisis or emotional distress.

emergencyResources.name: Crisis Text Line
emergencyResources.description: Free crisis counseling via text message.

emergencyResources.name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
emergencyResources.description: Support for victims of domestic violence.

emergencyResources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
emergencyResources.description: Free referral and information service for substance abuse and mental health.

emergencyResources.name: Veterans Crisis Line
emergencyResources.description: Crisis support for veterans and their families.
